ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Subject group as defined in the study
Notes: bn= Bulimia Nervosa; hc= Healthy Control

ElementDescription: The fasted and fed-state of subject
Notes: fasted= fasted state, no standardized meal before MRI scan; fed= fed state, ate the standardized meal before MRI scan

ElementDescription: Run number
Trial number
Stimulus type
Notes: 1= Go trial where a button should be pressed; 2= Stop trial where the button should not be pressed

ElementDescription: Button that was pressed by participant
Button that should be pressed for a correct response
The difficulty level of stop signal
Notes: 0= Go trial with no level; 1= Closest to the mean response time, most difficult condition; 6= Farthest from the mean response time, easiest condition

ElementDescription: fMRI trigger time (relative to the start of the EPrime experiment)
Run end time (relative to the start of the EPrime experiment)
Go signal start time (relative to the start of the EPrime experiment)
Go signal end time (relative to the start of the EPrime experiment)
Stop signal start time (relative to the start of the EPrime experiment)
Stop signal end time (relative to the start of the EPrime experiment)
Response time for each trial (relative to the start of the EPrime experiment)
